description: CO2-eq burden and cost breakdown for recycling aluminium (remelting scrap) using hydropower electricity, compared against primary production and the current market price. Sourced findings tagged 'measured value'; conjectures tagged 'assumed value' with numeric confidence.

words.name: aluminium recycling
words.description: Secondary aluminium production by remelting scrap, as opposed to primary smelting from bauxite

words.name: primary aluminium
words.description: Aluminium produced from bauxite via the Hall-Heroult electrolysis process

words.name: secondary aluminium
words.description: Aluminium produced from scrap by remelting

words.name: hydropower electricity
words.description: Electricity generated from hydroelectric plants, used here as the assumed sole electricity source

words.name: CO2-eq burden
words.description: Greenhouse gas emissions expressed as carbon dioxide equivalents

words.name: conversion cost
words.description: Cost of transforming scrap into finished secondary aluminium, excluding the metal value of the scrap input

words.name: scrap input cost
words.description: Purchase cost of aluminium scrap feedstock, indexed to the LME price

words.name: specific electricity demand
words.description: Electrical energy required per tonne of output

words.name: melt loss
words.description: Material lost as dross and oxidation during remelting, expressed as a mass fraction

words.name: LME 3-month price
words.description: London Metal Exchange three-month closing reference price for primary aluminium

words.name: regional premium
words.description: Surcharge on top of the LME base for physical delivery, e.g. duty-paid European billet premium

words.name: gas-fired furnace
words.description: Remelting furnace heated by natural gas combustion rather than electricity

words.name: electric furnace
words.description: Remelting furnace where all process heat is supplied electrically

words.name: per tonne
words.description: Unit basis: per metric tonne of output

words.name: kg CO2-eq per tonne
words.description: Kilograms of CO2 equivalent per tonne of aluminium output

words.name: t CO2-eq per tonne
words.description: Tonnes of CO2 equivalent per tonne of aluminium output

words.name: MWh per tonne
words.description: Megawatt-hours of electricity per tonne of output

words.name: g CO2-eq per kWh
words.description: Grams of CO2 equivalent per kilowatt-hour of electricity (lifecycle emission factor)

words.name: USD per tonne
words.description: US dollars per metric tonne

words.name: EUR per tonne
words.description: Euros per metric tonne

words.name: energy ratio recycling to primary
words.description: Energy used for recycling as a fraction of energy used for primary production

values.notes: Recycling uses about 5% of the energy of primary production (Hall-Heroult ~14,000 kWh/t).
Derived from ~5% of ~14 MWh/t primary; remelting electricity typically 0.6-0.7 MWh/t. Range 0.6-0.7.
Hall-Heroult electrolysis requires approximately 14,000 kWh per tonne.
Lifecycle emission factor for hydropower, typical range 10-24 gCO2eq/kWh; midpoint used.
Hypothetical case: ALL energy electric from hydropower, only material/dross losses remain. Range 50-150 kg/t.
Electricity-only share at 0.65 MWh/t and 17 gCO2eq/kWh. Range ~7-16 kg/t. Excludes non-electric process emissions.
Realistic full balance for secondary aluminium with gas-fired remelt furnace. Range ~300-600 kg/t. Contrast to the hydropower electric case.

source.name: International Aluminium Institute global average (widely cited industry figure)

values.notes: Global average primary aluminium footprint. Hydropower-based primary (Norway/Iceland) ~4-6 t/t.
3-month closing price (day-delayed). Down ~12% over the preceding four weeks after Gulf de-escalation; early-June high was 3752 USD/t.

source.name: London Metal Exchange LME Aluminium Alloy

values.notes: LME Aluminium Alloy 3-month closing price; cast alloys can trade above primary due to tightness.
Regional premium typically 80-300 USD/t (e.g. ~210 US Midwest, ~180 Japan CIF). Midpoint used.
Scrap typically 70-95% of LME depending on quality. At LME ~3150 that is ~2200-3000 USD/t. Midpoint used.
Total conversion margin excluding metal value: energy ~40-60, melt loss, labour, salts, pretreatment, maintenance. Range ~150-400 EUR/t.
Electricity cost component of conversion: 0.65 MWh/t at ~60-100 EUR/MWh industrial. Small share of total conversion cost.
Dross and oxidation loss during remelting, typically 2-8%. Midpoint used; often the largest hidden cost block.
